spec_notes: Modelled on CA sibling structure (ABC family) with Connecticut-specific data swaps from compliance-cache.
ABC prong text quoted verbatim from Conn. Gen. Stat. § 31-222(a)(1)(B)(ii); note disjunctive Prong B EITHER/OR wording that differentiates Connecticut from California's strict conjunctive Prong B.
Three-test agency split (UI / workers' comp / income tax) is the headline Connecticut distinctive — three agencies can reach three different answers on the same engagement.

No state-specific civil-penalty figure equivalent to California § 226.8; CT penalties under § 31-225a cover failure to register / pay UI taxes with interest at 1.0% per month, four-year lookback under § 31-273.
Federal US DOL 2024 final rule overlays for FLSA purposes only; does not override Connecticut state ABC test for UI.
Hero photo reused from CT sibling page to maintain visual consistency of the Connecticut family.
